items.situation: two students chatting about a pottery class one of them took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally finished that beginner pottery class at the community center. We made a full set of mugs on the wheel.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That sounds great! Was it hard? I always thought spinning clay looked kind of tricky.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the wheel was the tough part. But the instructor was super patient, and by the last session I made four mugs I'm actually proud of.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nice. Do they do other stuff too, like painting?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, they've got a watercolor class starting next month. You should sign up. I'll send you the link tonight.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You know what, I might. I've been meaning to try something creative for a while now.

questions.stem: What did the woman make in her class?

options.A: A set of mugs on the pottery wheel
options.B: A watercolor painting of the center
options.C: A large clay bowl for the instructor
options.D: A set of plates and cups by hand

questions.explanation: 女生明确说她在陶艺课上用转盘做了一整套马克杯（"a full set of mugs on the wheel"、"four mugs"），所以A正确。B是水彩课，那是下个月才开、她没上过，属于wrong_detail混淆。C的碗和给老师从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D把"手工"和"盘子"张冠李戴，她是用转盘做马克杯，属于wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Take the same pottery class next term
options.B: Ask the woman to teach him at home
options.C: Consider signing up for the watercolor class
options.D: Buy the woman's mugs from her

questions.explanation: 男生对女生提到的下月水彩课回应"I might"并说自己一直想尝试有创意的东西，暗示他很可能会去报名水彩课，所以C正确。A把陶艺课误当成他要上的，但他感兴趣的是水彩课，属于over_inference。B让女生在家教他从未提及，属于unrelated_next_step。D买马克杯对话里完全没有基础，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: a student asking a shop about repairing a cracked phone screen

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I dropped my phone this morning and the screen is completely cracked. Can you fix it here?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, we replace screens all the time. This model's a common one, so we've got the part in stock.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh good. How much, and how long will it take?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Eighty dollars. Normally it's ready in two hours, but our technician is out until three today, so it won't be done before four.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Four? Hmm, I have a class across town at two. I can't really wait around here.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: No worries. Just leave it with me and give me your number. I'll text you the moment it's finished.

questions.stem: Why will the repair take longer than usual today?

options.A: The replacement part is out of stock
options.B: The technician is not in until three
options.C: The shop is closing early today
options.D: The woman arrived too late in the day

questions.explanation: 男员工说通常两小时能修好，但"our technician is out until three today"，所以要到四点才行，B正确。A与对话相反，员工明说"we've got the part in stock"，属于reversed_situation。C店铺提早关门从未提及，属于related_but_not_stated。D把延迟归咎于女生来得晚，但延迟原因是技师不在，属于misattributed。

questions.stem: How will the woman find out her phone is ready?

options.A: She will wait at the shop until four
options.B: She will call the shop after her class
options.C: She will receive an email confirmation
options.D: She will get a text message from the man

questions.explanation: 男员工说"I'll text you the moment it's finished"，所以女生会通过短信得知，D正确。A女生已明说她两点有课不能在店里等，属于rejected_option。B由女生打电话过去与员工主动短信通知的安排相反，属于rejected_option。C邮件确认从未提及，是把常见通知方式套错，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two friends planning a birthday surprise for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So Dana's birthday is Friday. I was thinking we could throw her a surprise party after her seminar ends.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Love it. She'd never see it coming. Where were you thinking, the common room in our dorm?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Actually, that's booked. But I reserved the rooftop lounge on the fifth floor. It's got string lights and everything.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll handle the cake, then. She loves that lemon one from the bakery on Elm Street.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. Can you text everyone the plan? Just make sure nobody mentions it to Dana beforehand.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely. I'll set up a group chat without her and send out all the details tonight.

questions.stem: Where will the surprise party be held?

options.B: At the bakery on Elm Street

questions.explanation: 女生说她订了"the rooftop lounge on the fifth floor"，所以C正确。A公共休息室因被学习小组预订而放弃，属于rejected_option/wrong_detail。B Elm街的面包店只是买蛋糕的地方，不是聚会地点，属于misattributed。D研讨室只是Dana下课的地方，从未作为聚会地点，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Create a group chat that excludes Dana
options.C: Tell Dana about the party plan

questions.explanation: 男生说"I'll set up a group chat without her and send out all the details tonight"，所以A正确。B场地已由女生预订，不是男生的任务，属于misattributed。C与"确保没人提前告诉Dana"直接相反，属于reversed_situation。D取消学习小组预订从未提及，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm so tired of my playlist. It's the same three pop singers on repeat, and none of it excites me anymore.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I know that feeling. Have you tried branching into anything with actual instruments? Like some indie folk or jazz?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Not really. I wouldn't even know where to start looking.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: There's this app that builds you a mix based on one artist you already like. I found half my favorite bands that way.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's clever. So it just kind of expands outward from what I know?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. I'll send you the name after lunch. Start with one folk artist and just let it run for a week.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to make a playlist for a party
options.B: Why pop music has gotten worse lately
options.C: Which instruments are hardest to learn
options.D: Ways to discover new kinds of music

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女生厌倦了老一套流行歌、男生建议用一款app发现新曲风展开，主旨是如何发现新的音乐类型，D正确。A为聚会做歌单从未提及，属于wrong_topic。B流行音乐"变差"没人这么说，女生只是听腻了，属于reversed_situation。C乐器只是被顺带提到，且并非讨论哪种最难学，属于too_narrow。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Delete all the pop songs from her playlist
options.B: Try the app the man recommends
options.C: Sign up for a music theory course
options.D: Ask the man to make her a playlist

questions.explanation: 男生说午饭后会把app名字发给她，还让她从一位民谣歌手开始试用一周，女生反应积极，所以她最可能去试那款app，B正确。A删掉所有流行歌是极端化推断，对话只说她听腻了，属于over_inference。C报音乐理论课从未提及，属于unrelated_next_step。D让男生帮她做歌单与app自动生成的方案不符，属于unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two students comparing notes on a mystery novel they both read

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finish that mystery novel for lit class? The one set on the island?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did, last night. That ending totally caught me off guard. I never suspected the lighthouse keeper.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right? I was sure it was the doctor the whole time. The author really fooled me.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Same. Though I thought the middle dragged a bit. All those pages describing the storm.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, I skimmed some of that. Anyway, we should compare our essay ideas before Thursday's discussion.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. Let's grab coffee tomorrow and go over our arguments together.

questions.stem: Who turned out to be responsible in the novel?
questions.explanation: 男生说结局出人意料，"I never suspected the lighthouse keeper"，说明真凶是灯塔看守人，A正确。B医生是女生一直怀疑但猜错的对象，属于wrong_detail。C作者是写书的人而非书中人物，属于misattributed。D"storm survivor"从未出现，只是借用了暴风雨这个词，属于related_but_not_stated。

questions.stem: What will the two students most likely do tomorrow?

options.C: Meet for coffee to discuss their essays
options.D: Ask the professor to extend the deadline

questions.explanation: 男生说"Let's grab coffee tomorrow and go over our arguments"，女生也同意，所以两人明天最可能一起喝咖啡讨论论文，C正确。A重读暴风雨章节与他们都嫌那部分拖沓、想略过相反，属于rejected_option。B提前交论文从未提及，他们只是先对想法，属于unrelated_next_step。D请老师延期毫无依据，属于over_inference。
